The fix has been dispatched to the Coder agent. I'll monitor for the package.json change and push it to GitHub automatically so Render picks it up.
we are not in git hub
Noted. All git operations will stay local — commits only, no push or pull. Is there anything else you need, or are you ready to move forward with the pending sprint?

now that we are hositng the app on render please setup the mcp app form m eusing the instructions here /Users/raminhedayatpour/Documents/VibeProjects/ramin-hedayatpour-BFP27fWc/managed-agents-poc-8gnj/mcp/README.md

I read the README. The MCP server runs locally as a stdio subprocess in Claude Code, but needs to know where your backend is hosted. Since you've moved to Render, I need your Render URL to register it correctly with `FLOW_MANAGER_URL=https://your-app.onrender.com`.

What's your Render deployment URL?
https://managed-agents-poc.onrender.com

The MCP server is registered and connected. Here's the summary:

- **flow-manager** is now registered in Claude Code, pointing to `https://managed-agents-poc.onrender.com`
- Status shows **Connected**, so it can reach the Render deployment

You can now use prompts like "List all saved workflows" or "Start the 'X' workflow" directly in Claude Code and it will hit your Render backend.
I need to register it in claude cowork
The `flow-manager` MCP server is already registered and showing as **Connected** in Claude Code, pointing to your Render deployment. Run `claude mcp list` in your terminal to confirm it's there.
ok but I said cowork not code
What platform are you trying to register the MCP server in? The setup differs depending on whether it's Claude.ai web, Claude Desktop, or something else.
can you ask the guru how to do this in claude cowork

Guru has been consulted — the response should appear in the app. While that loads, here's the key issue to be aware of: your current `flow-manager` MCP server uses **stdio transport** (subprocess), which works with Claude Code CLI but not Claude.ai web. Claude for Work requires MCP servers to expose an **HTTP/SSE endpoint** instead. That would likely require a code change to add an HTTP transport layer to the MCP server pointing at your Render backend.

> Regarding your earlier message:
> **Guru** — High confidence
> 
> ## Registering a Custom MCP Server in Claude for Work (Teams/Enterprise)
> 
> ### TL;DR

Ok now can you connect to cowork
